headline: Motion
with
intent.
blocks.subhead: Five years building digital experiences for gaming’s biggest names, with motion at the centre. Motion graphics, 3D and WebGL that look good and run smoothly where it counts.

blocks.label: The practice
heading: Motion at the
centre.
paragraphs: Over the past five years I’ve worked with some of gaming’s most high-profile clients on experiences where motion carries the story. Along the way that has meant motion graphics, 3D modelling and WebGL production, and learning to make work that behaves itself in real-world conditions.
Outside client work, the pull is always toward hard sci-fi and cinematic environments, real skies, atmosphere and scale, and how far one person alone in Blender can push toward that feeling.

blocks.caption: Motion study, a vehicle composited into a real dusk sky.

blocks.label: APX
heading: Precise,
grounded.
paragraphs: Recent work for APX focused on motion that felt precise, confident and technologically grounded. Animation there is a way of guiding attention, reinforcing the brand and lending the product a sense of momentum.

blocks.caption: APX, motion in the brand system.

blocks.label: Real-time
heading: Optimised for
the browser.
paragraphs: Not every study is built to sit and look pretty. Some are built specifically for real-time web performance, geometry optimised, vertex counts reduced, textures compressed with care so load times stay quick and the image stays sharp.

blocks.caption: Real-time studies, built for the browser.
The Signal, a live scene, modelled, lit and built to run at 120Hz.

blocks.label: Direction
heading: Concept to
delivery.
paragraphs: For the Tezotopia trailer I led art direction and momentum, guiding external animators, shaping pacing and consistency, and assembling sound and music to give the piece weight.

A second piece for APX took a CAD model and rebuilt it as a fully lit, textured 3D asset, then opened up the shell to reveal the engineering underneath, using motion to explain the product rather than just show it off.

blocks.caption: APX, a CAD model textured, lit and opened up to show its workings.
